source.description: Six-panel pencil-sketch contact sheet generated during the July 2 cleanup and saved under drafts/summer-sun-hat-ribbon/ before cropping.
source.notes: Promoted after the owner asked to stop leaving generated images unused.

process_strategy: The contact sheet builds the same hat from construction ellipses through brim, crown, ribbon, bow, weave marks, and final graphite/color cleanup.

final_elements.name: wide floppy brim ellipse
final_elements.description: A broad tilted oval brim with slightly uneven, handmade edges.

final_elements.name: rounded hat crown
final_elements.description: A rounded crown rising from the middle of the brim.

final_elements.name: brim contour and underside
final_elements.description: The brim is refined with a front lip and a visible underside curve.

final_elements.name: ribbon band
final_elements.description: A band wrapping around the base of the crown.

final_elements.name: side bow and ribbon tails
final_elements.description: A small bow on one side with two soft ribbon tails.

final_elements.name: woven straw texture
final_elements.description: Loose curved hatch marks across the crown and brim.

final_elements.name: soft shadow and restrained color
final_elements.description: Light tan straw color, a warmer ribbon tint, and a small cast shadow.

frames.visible_job: Draws the wide brim ellipse, center guide, and rounded crown guide.
Turns the guide oval into a floppy brim with a front lip and underside curve.
Refines the crown and adds the ribbon band around its base.
Adds the side bow and two ribbon tails attached to the existing band.
Adds woven straw marks, light tan and ribbon color, and a soft cast shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens keeper contours, clarifies the bow and weave marks, and deepens existing restrained color and shadow.

rejection_checks: No bow, ribbon band, brim lip, weave texture, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same hat angle, scale, and placement as the step frames.
